title: CI: add attributes check run
body:
Related to: https://github.com/verilator/verilator/pull/3608

This PR adds ``clang_check_attributes.py`` that is using clang python bindings to make sure that if function is marked as ``VL_MT_SAFE`` it calls only functions that are also marked as ``VL_MT_SAFE``. For now it doesn't fail the CI run, but when all functions will satisfy this condition, it can be changed.

This CI run checks for 2 things:
 * if function is marked as ``VL_MT_SAFE``, it only calls other functions marked as ``VL_MT_SAFE``, if it is not the case, it prints tree of calls that leads to this unsafe call

 * if function is marked as ``VL_MT_START``, it prints all function calls to help finding out what functions are called
 
 I'm open to suggestions, I'm especially not sure if we want to print whole callstack of ``VL_MT_SAFE`` in the CI, but this feature is very helpful when developer wants to add new function that will run in parallel context.
 
 Before merge I will remove 6955e4dc074d9f86a9dcb2a42c0c21660e9ab0a3 commit, It was only added to showcase how ``VL_MT_START`` feature will look like.

Signed-off-by: Kamil Rakoczy <krakoczy@antmicro.com>
